# hubspot_pipeline (Resource)

Manages a pipeline and its exclusively owned writable stages.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hubspot_pipeline" "sales" {
  object_type = "deals"
  label       = "Sales pipeline"

  stages = {
    qualification = {
      label    = "Qualification"
      metadata = { probability = "0.1" }
    }
    closed_won = {
      label    = "Closed won"
      metadata = { probability = "1.0" }
    }
  }
}

resource "hubspot_pipeline" "support" {
  object_type = "tickets"
  label       = "Support pipeline"

  stages = {
    open = {
      label    = "Open"
      metadata = { ticketState = "OPEN" }
    }
    closed = {
      label    = "Closed"
      metadata = { ticketState = "CLOSED" }
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `label` (String) Pipeline display label.
- `object_type` (String) Exact HubSpot CRM object type API identifier.
- `stages` (Attributes Map) Complete set of writable stages, keyed by stable local identity. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--stages))

### Optional

- `display_order` (Number) HubSpot display order; defaults to -1.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Canonical object_type/pipeline_id identity.

<a id="nestedatt--stages"></a>
### Nested Schema for `stages`

Required:

- `label` (String) Stage display label.

Optional:

- `display_order` (Number) HubSpot display order; defaults to -1.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Object-specific stage metadata.

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) HubSpot-generated stage ID.
- `write_permissions` (String) HubSpot stage write-permission classification.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
tofu import hubspot_pipeline.sales 'deals/<pipeline-id>'
```
